title: dodecatheon pulchellum var. macrocarpum
intro: Dodecatheon pulchellum var. macrocarpum, also known as the western or tall pygmy shootingstar, is a perennial plant species native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Native American and Chinese herbal practices, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with the use of Dodecatheon pulchellum var. macrocarpum include its ability to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified practitioner.
